steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows 'the work of Slovak' and precedes 'who aptly describes her photos...' The blank must supply a noun phrase that (1) names the person and (2) is punctuated correctly before the non-restrictive relative clause 'who...'.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘photographer Mária Švarbová,’: The common noun 'photographer' is followed directly by the proper name (no comma between them), and a comma after the full noun phrase correctly sets off the non-restrictive relative clause. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘photographer Mária Švarbová’: A non-restrictive relative clause identifying a unique named person requires a comma before 'who'; omitting it is a punctuation error. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘photographer, Mária Švarbová’: A proper name in apposition to a common noun is not separated by a comma; inserting one is incorrect. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘photographer, Mária Švarbová,’: Same error as C. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
